step.title: Command Identity
step.goal: Define the command's identity: name, description, and optional agent/argument hints. The description is critical for discoverability.

substep.title: Command Name
substep.action:
Confirm or refine the command name:
Rules: - Lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens only - No spaces or underscores - Short and memorable (1-3 words) - Verb-first when possible (/commit, /review, /sync)

substep.title: Description
substep.action:
Write a clear, actionable description:
- Start with a verb (Creates, Validates, Runs) - Include key functionality - Mention when to use it - Keep under 100 characters for display
substep.ask:
Write a description for this command:
Format: "[Verb] [what it does] [optional: when/how]"
Examples: - "Creates git commits with optional message or file filter" - "Addresses GitHub PR review comments using the pr-review skill" - "Validates YAML files for syntax and schema compliance"

substep.title: Agent Assignment
substep.action:
Determine if this command should be assigned to a specific agent:
- Leave blank: Any agent can run it - Specify agent: That agent handles execution
Agent assignment (the instructions.md frontmatter `agent:`) affects which agent processes the command; visibility is determined by the config's `destination` ("." = global; an agent name = that agent).
substep.ask:
Should this command be assigned to a specific agent for execution?
Examples: - agent: build → Build agent handles execution - (blank) → Current agent handles it

substep.title: Argument Hint
substep.action:
If the command accepts arguments, provide a hint:
Format: "[required] [optional]" - Square brackets for optional - Angle brackets for required
This appears in command help to guide users.
substep.ask:
Does this command accept arguments? If so, what's the hint?
Examples: - "[optional: message, files, or instructions]" - "<pr-number>" - "[path]" - (leave blank if no arguments)

substep.title: Save Identity

rules.rule: Use lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens only

section.title: Description Best Practices

rules.rule: Start with a verb (Creates, Validates, Runs, Addresses)
Be specific about what it does

section.example: "Creates git commits with optional message, file filter, or instructions" "Validates YAML files for syntax and schema compliance"
"A command for doing commits" (vague) "This command..." (starts with "This")

section.title: Agent Assignment
section.description: Specifying an agent routes command execution to that agent. Leave blank if the current agent should handle it.

when-to-assign.rule: Command should always be handled by one agent
Agent has specialized tools for this task

section.example: /commit → agent: build (build agent has git tools) /research → (no agent, current handles it)
